explanation: By changing the current working directory first and using a relative path, the actual command line for regsvr32.exe will simply be 'regsvr32.exe payload.dll'. This avoids the literal string ':\ProgramData\' from appearing in the regsvr32 command line, bypassing the selection_cli criteria.

Using the Windows 8.3 short path representation for the directory. 'C:\PROGRA~3\' (which commonly resolves to C:\ProgramData\) does not match the exact substring ':\ProgramData\' expected by the Sigma rule.

Using directory traversal notation. The rule looks for the exact string ':\ProgramData\'. By using '..\ProgramData\', the executable still resolves to the correct path, but the command line string signature is broken.

Placing quotation marks around the directory name disrupts the exact string match. The command line parser will successfully load the DLL, but the string in the log will be '"C:\ProgramData"\' instead of 'C:\ProgramData\', causing the contains check to fail.
